title: thalictrum sphaerocarpum subsp. udum
intro: Thalictrum sphaerocarpum subsp. udum, also known as Meadow Rue, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Ranunculaceae, characterized by its delicate white or purple flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and exhibit antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Thalictrum sphaerocarpum subsp. udum include its potential to alleviate menstrual cramps, reduce fever, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, or by using it as a tincture in combination with other medicinal herbs.
